bullets.text: Annual underwriting margin 7.6% — nearly double the 4% target; GAAP CR 92.5 for Personal Lines; Commercial Auto CR 90.9; record net income $667.3M; ROE 19.3% well above 15% target.

NPW grew 30% to approximately $9.5B; Personal Lines +29% ($8.4B); Commercial Auto surpassed $1B written (+50%+); Direct +36%; Agency +26%; PIF growth 22% Personal Lines, 38% Commercial Auto — every market growing.

Personal Lines PIF +22%; Special Lines PIF +20%; policy life expectancy extended significantly across all lines and channels — driven by market conditions, electronic payment plans, and growing customer retention culture.

$400M debt issued November 2002; invested assets $10.3B (up from $8.2B); shareholders' equity $3.77B (+$500M+); portfolio total return 5.5% FTE; common stocks returned -21.5% while fixed income returned +10.1%.

Unexpected and exceptional drop in claim frequency benefited results; relatively few catastrophes in 2002; competitors withdrawing, constraining new business, or filing for double-digit rate increases — conditions Progressive benefited from directly.

Claims Service Centers (Concierge model) piloted — 7 sites operational with 20 more planned for 2003; 3,000+ new claim representatives hired and trained; agent technology enabling 60%+ of policy changes directly; expense ratio at or below agent-channel competitors.

#3 in U.S. personal auto at 5.9% share — climbed 31 places in 20 years; top 15 market share consolidated to 70% (from 54% in 1972); market leader took significant corrective action; Progressive gained while others constrained.

Homeowners insurance entry ended in 2002 — not meeting objectives; bundling challenge acknowledged; Segway Human Transporter became first-ever insured product; Commercial Auto separate reporting introduced to highlight its growing importance.

High Expectations TV campaign promoting comparison shopping and Internet use advanced; Direct Internet sales growing — expected to be sustained long-term growth catalyst; media costs favorable in 2002 with moderate future firming expected.

Monthly financial reporting established as new standard of disclosure — accident year vs. calendar year reconciliation added; agent electronic access to customer data expanded; real-time data validation at quote improving accuracy; new billing system months away from deployment.
